title: North AmericaГўв‚¬в„ўs Гўв‚¬ЛњFirstГўв‚¬в„ў Hydrogen Passenger Train Enters Service in California
content: California has launched North AmericaГўв‚¬в„ўs first hydrogen-powered passenger train, marking a milestone in U.S. rail decarbonization. Built by Stadler in 2024, the hybrid hydrogen fuel cell-battery train will run up to 16 trips daily on the nine-mile San BernardinoГўв‚¬вЂњRedlands University route, following months of testing for Metrolink and federal compliance. Based on StadlerГўв‚¬в„ўs FLIRT design, the train offers a 380-mile range and speeds up to 79 mph. The San Bernardino County Transport Authority (SBCTA) celebrated the debut with free city metro rides, highlighting the project as part of its commitment to cleaner air and advanced transit technologies. SBCTA Board President Rick Denison called the train Гўв‚¬Е“proofГўв‚¬Вќ of regional leadership in adopting sustainable transport. While hydrogen trains have been in use across Europe for years, the fragmented and largely freight-owned U.S. rail system has been slow to embrace zero-emission technologies, with little existing electrification. StadlerГўв‚¬в„ўs Commercial Project Manager Kaden Killpack noted that unlike Europe, where overhead power lines provide a ready path to zero emissions, U.S. regions like San Bernardino needed alternativesГўв‚¬вЂќmaking hydrogen Гўв‚¬Е“a lot of senseГўв‚¬Вќ as a practical solution. The project positions Southern California at the forefront of clean rail innovation, bridging the technological gap with Europe and signaling growing momentum for hydrogen in American transit systems.
author: Analysts Account
